thesis_status_reasoning: The core thesis that RWDN possesses robust structural integrity and sufficient redundancy is categorically falsified. Multiple kill switches have been simultaneously breached: System Pressure (24 PSI) is critically below the 40 PSI regulatory threshold (Criterion 4), Sector B hydrant pressure (12 PSI) has fallen below the 20 PSI fire flow minimum (Criterion 3), Pump 2 is operating at 165% load with 85 Hz vibration indicating active destructive cavitation (Criteria 1 and 2), and Reservoir Level at 96.5% with collapsed system pressure confirms the Divergent Inventory Paradox (Criterion 5). Three of four pumps are non-operational, eliminating the claimed mechanical redundancy.
action_reasoning: Pump 2 is experiencing catastrophic mechanical failure at 165% load and 85 Hz vibration, risking imminent destruction that could cascade through the grid. The system has already lost sanitary integrity and fire protection capability; continuing operation only accelerates infrastructure damage without providing adequate service.

key_signals.signal: Pump 2 operating at 165% load with 85 Hz vibration
System pressure at 24 PSI, 40% below regulatory minimum of 40 PSI
Sector B hydrant pressure at 12 PSI, 40% below fire flow minimum of 20 PSI
Reservoir level at 96.5% while system pressure collapsed, confirming supply-distribution decoupling
Only 1 of 4 pumps operational, with emergency cross-connection to neighboring grid activated

biggest_uncertainty: Whether emergency cross-connection to neighboring grid can stabilize pressure before Pump 2 catastrophic failure propagates additional damage to transmission mains or causes complete grid collapse
